title: NOC 12203 – Assessors, Business Valuators, and Appraisers | GTR Immigration
description: Assessors, Business Valuators, and Appraisers play a vital role in Canada’s real estate and financial sectors by determining property and business values for taxation, lending, legal, and commercial purposes. Classified under NOC 12203, this occupation continues to experience strong demand nationwide and offers practical immigration pathways toward Permanent Residence (PR) through Canada’s skilled worker programs and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).

classification.teer: 2 – Occupations usually require a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more years; or supervisory occupations
classification.major_group: 122 – Accounting, insurance, and related business administrative occupations
classification.minor_group: 1220 – Accounting, insurance, and related business administrative occupations

common_job_titles: Accredited appraiser Business valuator Chartered business valuator (CBV) Property assessor Property valuator Real estate appraiser Real estate analyst
duties: Evaluate land, buildings, and equipment for taxation or planning purposes
Analyze sales records, maps, zoning data, and engineering documents
Explain valuation processes to property owners
Review financial records to estimate market value or competitiveness
Prepare reports for litigation, estate planning, and insurance
Testify as expert witnesses before courts or regulators
Appraise residential, commercial, and industrial property and equipment
Prepare valuation reports for lenders, courts, insurers, and buyers
employment_requirements: College diploma in municipal assessment and provincial accreditation for assessors
Business valuators generally require accounting or commerce education plus certification from the Canadian Institute of Chartered Business Valuators
Appraisers usually require post-secondary training or relevant experience
In Quebec, membership with OEAQ is required for chartered appraisers
Real estate appraisers often require Appraisal Institute of Canada accreditation
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create 8,200 new positions for Assessors, Business Valuators, and Appraisers, compared with 7,900 job seekers—suggesting balanced but favorable opportunities for experienced overseas applicants.
additional_information: Continued hiring in real estate valuation and taxation servicesDemand driven by property transactions and regulatory requirementsEligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs)Employer-supported immigration optionsAnnual earnings typically ranging from $38,500 to $98,000 CAD
